summary: Matched the previously stubbed vi cutscene function fn_8031EFE4 and updated its header prototype from placeholder UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to a typed void function taking HSD_GObj*. The implementation advances a camera object animation, triggers vi_8031C9B4 events on specific animation frames, and calls lb_800145F4 plus gm_801A4B60 when the animation reaches its end frame. PR summary reports fn_8031EFE4 at 100% and vi0801 .sdata2 progress from 0 to 38.89%.
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder comment /// #fn_8031EFE4 in src/melee/vi/vi0801.c with a matching C implementation.
Added includes needed by the matched function: gm/gm_unsplit.h, lb/lb_00F9.h, baselib/aobj.h, baselib/cobj.h, and baselib/gobj.h.

Implemented fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj* gobj) by extracting the camera object with GET_COBJ(gobj), running HSD_CObjAnim(cobj), then checking cobj->aobj->curr_frame against literal frame values 1.0F, 30.0F, and 60.0F.
At curr_frame 1.0F or 30.0F, the function calls vi_8031C9B4(0xC, 0).
At curr_frame 60.0F, the function calls vi_8031C9B4(0x10, 0).
At the animation end frame, the function calls lb_800145F4() followed by gm_801A4B60().
Updated src/melee/vi/vi0801.h to include baselib/forward.h and changed fn_8031EFE4 from UNK_RET fn_8031EFE4(UNK_PARAMS) to void fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj*).
smart_moves: Used GET_COBJ and HSD_CObjAnim directly, which exposes the function as a camera-object animation callback rather than leaving it as an opaque cutscene stub.
Typed the public prototype in the header at the same time as the C implementation, reducing future placeholder propagation for vi0801.
Kept the header dependency light by adding baselib/forward.h there, while placing concrete baselib aobj/cobj/gobj includes in the .c file where fields and macros are needed.
Matched frame-driven behavior with explicit float literals 1.0F, 30.0F, and 60.0F, preserving likely codegen-sensitive comparisons against HSD_AObj curr_frame.
Recognized the end-of-animation branch by comparing curr_frame to cobj->aobj->end_frame, then sequencing cleanup/transition calls lb_800145F4 and gm_801A4B60.
decomp_lessons: For vi cutscene callbacks, inspect HSD_CObj and its HSD_AObj animation state; frame checks can reveal event triggers and completion transitions.
When replacing UNK_PARAMS prototypes, propagate the concrete baselib object type into the header with a forward declaration rather than over-including full baselib headers.
Camera animation callbacks may need both baselib animation calls and higher-level game/lb transition calls, so matching may require includes across vi, gm, lb, and baselib.
Frame equality checks in these cutscene functions may intentionally use exact float constants and should not be rewritten into ranges unless there is evidence.
End-frame logic can be expressed as curr_frame == end_frame when matching animation completion behavior.
naming_conventions: Function remains named fn_8031EFE4, indicating the PR matched behavior but did not identify a semantic name beyond the address-based placeholder.
Existing vi functions in the surrounding file use mixed prefixes such as un_8031F274_OnFrame and un_8031F07C_OnEnter, preserving decomp project callback suffix conventions.
Header comments retain address annotations such as /* 31EFE4 */ beside declarations.
Baselib types and macros use HSD_* naming: HSD_GObj, HSD_CObj, GET_COBJ, HSD_CObjAnim.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced exact typed access to cobj->aobj->curr_frame and cobj->aobj->end_frame, likely replacing opaque parameter handling with struct-field-based codegen.
Used float constants with F suffix, which is important for matching single-precision comparisons and .sdata2 constants.
The PR summary explicitly notes main/melee/vi/vi0801 .sdata2 progress from 0 to 38.89%, implying the added float literals contributed to matched read-only/sdata2 constants.
Kept branch structure simple as separate if statements rather than else-if, matching the shown logic where each frame condition is independently checked.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type fn_8031EFE4 or related vi cutscene callbacks.
Search vi0801 for other HSD_CObjAnim callbacks with frame-triggered vi_8031C9B4 calls to infer event IDs 0xC and 0x10.
Look for usages or definitions of gm_801A4B60 and lb_800145F4 to understand the cutscene transition/cleanup path.
Check subsequent vi0801 .sdata2 matching PRs for reuse of exact float frame constants.
